extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE attacked final climb with ROGLIČ, then forced ROGLIČ to lead on descent and out-positioned him in sprint finish.
tactical_patterns: Team Sky (KWIATKOWSKI/LANDA) set penultimate climb tempo 22km out, revealing defensive posture rather than controlling final climb.
ALAPHILIPPE forced ROGLIČ to take descent lead by sitting on, preserving positioning advantage for sprint finish.
QUINTANA dropped on explosive punchy final climb, confirming unsuitability to short steep efforts versus longer alpine tests.
Lotto-Jumbo and Quick-Step alternated lead duties on final climb approach, suggesting neither confident enough to commit solo.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: tactical maturity forcing ROGLIČ to lead descent while preserving sprint positioning shows race-winning craft
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: matched ALAPHILIPPE on decisive final climb attack but tactically outmaneuvered on descent lead-out
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: dropped when ALAPHILIPPE/ROGLIČ launched explosive attack, confirming weakness on short punchy finales versus longer climbs
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: chased ALAPHILIPPE/ROGLIČ group but couldn't close on descent, third-tier positioning
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: finished ahead of main group as Quick-Step backup, showing form depth
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: neutral: finished in lead group behind podium, solid positioning
rider_signals.IZAGIRRE Gorka: neutral: survived final climb in front group
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: worked penultimate climb tempo for Sky 22km out, standard domestique role
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: shared Sky tempo-setting 22km out before finale
